text.title: CPA Rollup Output Contract
text.summary: The rollup is a single weekly artifact (sheet + dashboard) with consistent columns across the team. Reports built outside the rollup are not used in budget decisions.

list.item: week — ISO week (yyyy-Www).
channel — single canonical name (Meta-paid, Google-search, Google-pmax, LinkedIn-paid, X-paid, Organic, Referral, Direct, etc.).
gross_spend_usd — incl. fees per r2.
self_reported_conversions — vendor's number, recorded for reference.
attributed_conversions — per the single attribution model (r1, r3).
view_through_count — separate, not in CPA denominator.
cpa_gross — gross_spend / attributed_conversions.
incremental_over_organic — attributed_conversions − channel-share-of-organic-baseline.
cpa_incremental — gross_spend / incremental_over_organic.
cohort_d30_retention, cohort_d60, cohort_d90.
notes — anomalies, suspensions, promotions.
Weekly rebuild + publish (script + dashboard refresh).
Attribution-policy file reviewed quarterly; changes are versioned.
Cohort retention requires >= 30 days of post-acquisition data — recent weeks show partial cohort with explicit "in progress" flag.

antipattern.description: CPA computed against self-reported conversions instead of attributed conversions.

antipattern.reason: Violates r2. CPA is rosier than reality.

antipattern.description: Organic baseline absent — paid channels evaluated against zero counterfactual.
antipattern.reason: Violates r4. Cannibalization of organic by branded paid is invisible.

antipattern.description: View-through conversions added to attributed total without an incrementality study.
antipattern.reason: View-through inflates the count without evidence. Keep as separate column.

list.item: One attribution model documented and used across the rollup.
Gross spend includes all channel-related fees.
Conversions deduplicated by customer ID.
Organic baseline row populated; CPA-incremental computed.
Cohort retention columns populated for cohorts old enough.
View-through stored separately from attributed conversions.
